original text:
Summary Heap-based buffer overflow Adobe Flash Player before 13.0.0.296 and 14.x through 18.x before 18.0.0.194 Windows and and before 11.2.202.468 Linux allows remote attackers execute arbitrary code via unspecified vectors, exploited the wild June 2015.
description This module exploits buffer overflow Adobe Flash Player when handling nellymoser encoded audio inside FLV video, exploited the wild June 2015. This module has been tested successfully on: Windows SP1 (32-bit), IE11 and Adobe Flash 18.0.0.160, Windows SP1 (32-bit), Firefox 38.0.5 and Adobe Flash 18.0.0.160, Windows 8.1, Firefox 38.0.0.0.160, Linux Mint "Rebecca" (32 bits), Firefox 33.0 and Adobe Flash 11.2.466, and Ubuntu 14.04.2 LTS, Firefox 35.01, and Adobe Flash 11.2.466. Note that this exploit effective against both CVE-2015-3113 and the earlier CVE-2015-3043, since CVE-2015-3113 effectively regression the same root cause CVE-2015-3043.
description The version Adobe Flash Player installed the remote Windows host equal prior version 18.0.0.161. is, therefore, affected remote code execution vulnerability due improper validation unspecified user-supplied input. remote attacker can exploit this, via specially crafted Flash content, cause heap-based buffer overflow, resulting the execution arbitrary code.
description Adobe Flash Player was updated 11.2.468, fixing security issue, where attackers could trigger heap overflow and could execute code. Note that Tenable Network Security has extracted the preceding description block directly from the SUSE security advisory. Tenable has attempted automatically clean and format much possible without introducing additional issues.
description The version Adobe Flash Player installed the remote Mac host equal prior version 18.0.0.
2.468, fixing security issue, where attackers could have triggered heap overflow and could have executed code. 4.html Note that Tenable Network Security has extracted the preceding description block directly from the SUSE security advisory.
description Adobe reports Adobe has released security updates for Adobe Flash Player for Windows, Macintosh and Linux. These updates address critical vulnerability (CVE-2015-3113) that could potentially allow attacker take control the affected system. Adobe aware reports that CVE-2015-3113 being actively exploited the wild via limited, targeted attacks. Systems running Internet Explorer for Windows and below, well Firefox Windows XP, are known targets.
description updated Adobe Flash Player package that fixes one security issue now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux and Supplementary. Red Hat Product Security has rated this update having Critical security impact. Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives detailed severity rating, available from the CVE link the References section. The flash-plugin package contains Mozilla Firefox compatible Adobe Flash Player web browser plug-in. flaw was found the way flash-plugin displayed certain SWF content. attacker could use this flaw create specially crafted SWF file that would cause flash-plugin crash or, potentially, execute arbitrary code when the victim loaded page containing the malicious SWF content. (CVE-2015-3113) All users Adobe Flash Player should install this updated package, which upgrades Flash Player version 11.2.468.
description The remote Windows host missing KB3074219.
description The remote host affected the vulnerability described GLSA-201507-13 (Adobe Flash Player: Multiple vulnerabilities) Multiple vulnerabilities have been discovered Adobe Flash Player. Please review the CVE identifiers referenced below for details. Impact remote attacker could possibly execute arbitrary code with the privileges the process, cause Denial Service condition, obtain sensitive information, bypass security restrictions. Workaround There known workaround this time.

original text:
3.8.2 NEW: Improved compatibility with UltraVNC SecureVNC DSM plug-in.
3.8.1 NEW: The ability align remote screen within ViewerX ActiveX control boundaries. See the new values that have been added the enum.
NEW: The NTLM login window pre-initialized with the current user name and domain credentials have been set via the API.
FIX: ViewerX makes the hosting application window active when connection uppon esteblishing connection.
3.7.5 FIX: 64-bit build may crash while perfoming UltraVNC NTLM authentication.
3.7.4 FIX: Cancel button not visible the password prompt screen.
3.7.3 NEW: Improved compatibility with Xvnc server.
3.7.2 NEW: Various rendering optimizations and fixes.
3.7.0 NEW: Implemented support for libjpeg-turbo. This allows accelerate decompression and processing jpeg images.
3.6.2 FIX: Stability and security fixes.
3.6.1 NEW: Support for unicode clipboard transfers when connected UltraVNC server.
3.5.9 FIX: rare occasions ViewerX crashes when remote server closes VNC connection.
3.5.8 FIX: Stability and security fixes.
3.5.7 FIX: Security vulnerability TLS heartbeat read overrun (CVE-2014-0160).
3.5.5 FIX: Windows compatibility fixes.
3.5.4 NEW: Improved support for handling so-called "dead" keys, for example accent key Spanish keyboard.
3.5.3 NEW: the Disconnect() method was called while asynchronous connection (ConnectAsync) still being established, would return immediately. Previously the Disconnect() method would hang until connection has been established.
FIX: Fixed memory leak.
3.5.2 NEW: Changed the way how ViewerX renders screen updates when the property set non-zero value.
FIX: Calling SetDormant(bool) method may result the application crash.
3.5.1 NEW: Added support for SecureVNC v2.3 DSM plugin.
FIX: Upon establishing connection scrollbars may not visible.
3.4.3 NEW: When connected UltraVNC server running Vista Windows7, the Auto Reconnect feature has been fine tuned better handle cases when UltraVNC server breaks connection upon user logoff.
FIX: Application may hang when Auto Reconnect window appears.
3.4.1 NEW: ConnectingText, ListeningText properties now support Unicode strings.
3.3.8 FIX: "Empty desktop size" error when accepting listening connection from establishing connection TightVNC server without password set.
FIX: event gets sent after Disconnected event.
3.3.7 NEW: Added support for sending Ctrl+Alt+Del command remote computer via Ctrl+Alt+End keyboard shortcut. See and properties for more information.
3.3.6 FIX: Minor bug fixes.
3.3.5.0 NEW: Added property. When the screen stretching mode enabled, this property allows you switch between GDI and GDI+ based screen scaling engine. Starting with this build, default, ViewerX uses GDI+ (Low Quality) scaling mode.
3.3.4.0 FIX: takes too long establish connection computer that has IPv4 and IPv6 addresses assigned.
3.3.1.0 NEW: ConnextXXX functions have been updated support Internet Protocol version (IPv6).
3.2.3.0 NEW: Support for legacy VNC servers (such RealVNC 3.3.7) that use RFB v3.3 protocol.
3.2.2.0 NEW: API: Event OnMouseMove allows tracking the mouse cursor movements.
3.2.1.0 NEW: Support for UltraVNC Server 1.0.9.5 with RFB protocol v3.8. This enables file-transfer with 1.0.9.5 servers and fixes "Unsupported security type" message that you would receive you had tried connect UltraVNC 1.0.9.5 with NTLM authentication.
3.1.6.0 sample project has been modified use ViewerX COM component.
3.1.4.0 CHANGED: was made compatible work with any VNC server. the previous builds was working with UltraVNC server only.
3.1.3.0 FIX: When using non-English keyboard layout some characters may appear question marks.
3.1.2.0 NEW: SecureVNC v2.2.4.0 DSM plug-in support. See the API reference interface for more details.
3.0.6.1 FIX: Mouse clicks handled incorrectly Chinese, Japanese Korean language set the default input method.
3.0.6.0 RTM NEW: Brand new rendering engine implementation. The engine uses less CPU and renders screen updates much faster comparing the previous versions.
NEW: Ability reconnect automatically VNC server the event network disconnection. support this feature new property and event have been added.
NEW: Added support for Ultra, ZYWRLE, and Cache encodings.
NEW: ColorDepth property allows specify the number colors used. Currently full colors, 256 colors, colors, and colors palettes are supported. This property deprecates the RestrictPixel property.
NEW: New events have been added that allow logging messages sent/received via UltraVNC chat.
NEW: method has been implemented. the previous builds this method was returning black&white image.
NEW: event Fired when ViewerX receives new "disable remote input" state from VNC server.
NEW: property has been added. sets the outer background color. This background color used paint the background when the ViewerX size larger than the remote screen.
FIX: Fixed several stability issues.
FIX: Screen longer updated after sending receiving files via UltraVNC file transfer.
FIX: application creates multiple ViewerX instances that use Tight encoding JPEG compression may cause random crashes.
FIX: Screen artifacts form horizontal lines may appear when ZlibHex encodings used.
FIX: File Transfer window appears instead the chat window when chat initiated from the server side.
2.9.7.0 NEW: ViewerX 64-bit version now available. The installer now installs 32-bit and 64-bit ViewerX binaries.
FIX: Fails establish connection via HTTP proxy.
2.9.6.3 FIX: multi-monitor configuration, "Enter Login Credentials" window was always displayed the primary monitor.
FIX: set VCT_NO_CURSOR(0) mouse cursor may disappear when hovering over scrollbars.
2.9.6.1 FIX: Moving mouse cursor with "screen stretching" mode enabled may leave trailing marks the screen.
2.9.6.0 NEW: sends key key down notifications remote VNC server.
2.9.5.5 NEW: Support for "per-user" COM registration.
FIX: Cannot open more than one ViewerX instance with DSM encryption enabled.
2.9.5.4 NEW: Enchanced support for UltraVNC 1.0.5.3 file transfers.
NEW API: Allows you switching between displays. UltraVNC server specific feature.
NEW API: Sets RC4 key form SAFEARRAY bytes.
NEW API: Sets RC4 key form HEX string. Each byte must represented two hexadecimal characters. the byte value can represented single hex character must padded with zero value. For example, 0F.
NEW API: Specifies which key storage ViewerX must use read RC4 key data. RC4 key can passed ViewerX path external RC4 file, binary SAFEARRAY, form HEX string.
2.9.5.1 FIX: ViewerX fails repaint itself properly when screen stretching mode turned on.
2.9.5.0 NEW: SOCKS5 proxy support.
NEW: Improved the error diagnostic case ViewerX was unable establish VNC connection.
2.8.7.0 FIX: Scrollbars aren't visible after establishing connection server with screen resolution larger than ViewerX window size.
FIX: Destroying ViewerX while VNC file transfer window visible may result crash.
FIX: rare occasions, ViewerX may hand while closing VNC connection.
2.8.5.0 NEW: event allows suppress the password prompt dialog.
FIX: Cannot move ViewerX Visual Basic designer.
2.8.1.0 NEW: Ability select "single window" (UltraVNC server only).
NEW: Enable/Disable remote input (UltraVNC server only).
2.7.6.0 NEW: Support for UltraVNC MSRC4 DSM plug-in v1.2.2.
2.7.5.0 FIX: Attempt establish connection via SOCKS5 proxy server crashes ViewerX.
2.7.4.0 NEW: Support for UltraVNC 1.0.2 NTLM (MSLogon v2) authentication.
2.6.10.0 Implemented workaround for Delphi forms bug: ViewerX doesn't send keyboard events when hosted inside Delphi application.
FIX: TightVNC and UltraVNC file transfers are broken.
2.6.8.0 NEW: UltraVNC DSM plug-in support.
NEW: UltraVNC Single-Click server support.
2.5.86.2 CHANGES: Starting from this build, the control intercepts Alt key presses and doesn't pass them the parent container.
2.5.0 FIX: Ctrl-C, Home, End and some other keys might not work when the control hosted inside Internet Explorer.
2.5.85.1 FIX: Fixes the UltraVNC file transfer.
FIX: uses default port value instead custom one.
2.5.83.0 NEW: Support for UltraVNC file transfer.
2.5.75.0 NEW: Support for UltraVNC Repeater proxy.
2.5.72.0 NEW: Better handling remote screen resolution changes. ServerDimension event now fired every screen resolution change.
FIX: Background painting problem errors ViewerX control window size bigger than remote screen size.
2.5.71.1 FIX: Visual Basic sample project fails instantiate ActiveX control.
FIX: Values ListeningText, ConnectingText properties are invalid when loading control from persistent storage.
2.5.0 NEW: Support for UltraVNC chat
NEW: Help file updated with interface description.
2.5.69.0 FIX: Artifacts when control Stretching mode.
2.5.68.0 FIX: Minor fixes file transfer dialog.
2.5.66.0 NEW: Synchronized with latest TightVNC 1.3dev6 release.
2.5.65.1 FIX: ViewerX might freeze VNC server goes down.
CHANGED: Rebuild with renewed software signing certificate.
2.5.61.0 NEW: Added support for the latest TightVNC 1.3dev5 and RealVNC 4.0 features.
2.3.45.0 FIX: ViewerX fails verify design-time license Windows 2000 based computers.
FIX: Internet Explorer stops resolving URLs after refreshing HTML page with ViewerX control.
2.3.41.5 CHANGES: Minor changes the control resizing handling.
2.3.40.3 FIX: some installations, ViewerX repositions itself the upper left corner inside container window.
FIX: When running inside Web Browser, ViewerX loses input focus after browser window has been restored from minimized state.
2.3.1 NEW: Help file updated with information about deploying ViewerX Web server.
NEW: PLK license file has been included installation package.
2.3.0 NEW: Listening VNC viewer has been implemented. Now ViewerX control can listen for incoming VNC server connections.
2.3.38.0 FIX: High CPU usage when working "stretched mode".

FIX: uses default port value instead custom one.
 NEW: Support for UltraVNC file transfer.
 NEW: Support for UltraVNC Repeater proxy.
 NEW: Better handling remote screen resolution changes. ServerDimension event now fired every screen resolution change.
FIX: Background painting problem errors ViewerX control window size bigger than remote screen size.
 FIX: Visual Basic sample project fails instantiate ActiveX control.
FIX: Values ListeningText, ConnectingText properties are invalid when loading control from persistent storage.
2.5.0 NEW: Support for UltraVNC chat
NEW: Help file updated with interface description.
 FIX: Artifacts when control Stretching mode.
 FIX: Minor fixes file transfer dialog.
 NEW: Synchronized with latest TightVNC 1.3dev6 release.
 FIX: ViewerX might freeze VNC server goes down.
CHANGED: Rebuild with renewed software signing certificate.
 NEW: Added support for the latest TightVNC 1.3dev5 and RealVNC 4.0 features.
 FIX: ViewerX fails verify design-time license Windows 2000 based computers.
FIX: Internet Explorer stops resolving URLs after refreshing HTML page with ViewerX control.
 CHANGES: Minor changes the control resizing handling.
 FIX: some installations, ViewerX repositions itself the upper left corner inside container window.
FIX: When running inside Web Browser, ViewerX loses input focus after browser window has been restored from minimized state.
2.3.1 NEW: Help file updated with information about deploying ViewerX Web server.
NEW: PLK license file has been included installation package.
2.3.0 NEW: Listening VNC viewer has been implemented. Now ViewerX control can listen for incoming VNC server connections.
 FIX: High CPU usage when working "stretched mode".
